summary: Regression tested the Casting Review architecture overhaul (Takes→Intro→Images ordering, renamable takes, retest/re-upload flow, competitive_brand + custom_answers visibility gating). Unit suite (test_casting_review.py + test_client_payload_isolation.py) — 18/18 PASS. New live-API integration suite (test_casting_review_live.py) — 9/10 PASS; 1 FAIL exposes a real contract bug: the admin edit endpoint Pydantic model rejects the per-question custom_answers visibility dict. Frontend admin UI loads (login → projects/overview render), and SubmissionPage.jsx is correctly wired with TakeRow, AddTakeSlot, patchTakeLabel, and data-testid='refine-submission-btn' for the retest CTA.

critical.issue: AdminSubmissionEditIn.field_visibility is typed Dict[str, bool] — rejects per-question custom_answers dict shape ({Q1: false, Q2: true}) with 422. Backend _submission_to_client_shape already handles per-question dicts, so the bug is only in the API input model. Spec explicitly requires this shape.

action_items: Relax AdminSubmissionEditIn.field_visibility type in /app/backend/core.py from Optional[Dict[str, bool]] to Optional[Dict[str, Any]] (or a Union[bool, Dict[str, bool]] value type) so the admin can persist the per-question custom_answers visibility dict — the rest of the pipeline already supports it (_submission_to_client_shape line 824 handles both bool and dict). Re-run /app/backend/tests/test_casting_review_live.py::test_public_link_custom_answers_modes after the fix.
critical_code_review_comments: core.py line 500: AdminSubmissionEditIn.field_visibility typed too strictly — blocks documented per-question custom_answers feature.

_submission_to_client_shape take-ordering uses a closure (_take_sort_key) that re-scans raw_media for every comparison — O(n^2) per submission. Fine at 5 takes max, but document or precompute the legacy-category index.
submission_upload: when a legacy take_1/2/3 fills a single-slot, the $pull happens BEFORE the $push in the same update — atomic. Good.
submission_delete_media does not 404 when mid is missing (just silently no-ops). Consider matching the PATCH semantics and returning 404 for consistency.

Re-upload flips status→'updated' for ANY submission in ('submitted','updated'), including 'image' uploads after finalize. That matches the spec ('any post-submit mutation'), but be aware an admin approving then a talent adding an image resets the decision — make sure the admin UI surfaces 'decision reset' messaging.
No max-label-length validation in submission_update_media — could accept arbitrarily long strings. Low risk but worth a 200-char cap.

success_rate.frontend: admin UI smoke OK (login + projects render)

test_credentials: admin@talentgram.com / Admin@123 (admin); raj@test.com / RajPass123! (team). Both confirmed working.
seed_data_creation: Test fixtures created and cleaned up: TEST_Casting_* projects, TEST_Link_* links, test_*@ex.com submissions. Post-run cleanup confirmed: 5 test links + 10 test submissions deleted. No leftover state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/test_casting_review.py tests/test_casting_review_live.py tests/test_client_payload_isolation.py -v`. test_casting_review_live.py hits the live preview backend and creates throwaway projects+links+submissions (cleanup is inline on project fixture but links teardown is implicit — re-run cleanup script in iteration_3 if re-testing repeatedly). The one failing test (test_public_link_custom_answers_modes) will pass once AdminSubmissionEditIn.field_visibility accepts a dict value type. The fixture `_prep_submission_for_link` uses tiny dummy mp4/jpg (safe for storage).
rca of the issue: The backend core layer supports the per-question custom_answers visibility contract end-to-end (DEFAULT_FIELD_VISIBILITY, _submission_to_client_shape with isinstance(ca_vis, dict) branch at core.py:824, CLIENT_ALLOWED_FIELDS allowlist). However, the *input* Pydantic model AdminSubmissionEditIn on core.py:500 constrains field_visibility to Dict[str, bool], so the admin cannot actually persist the dict shape through PUT /api/projects/{pid}/submissions/{sid}. Reproduction: login as admin → PUT /api/projects/{pid}/submissions/{sid} with body {"field_visibility": {"custom_answers": {"Q1": true}}} → 422 bool_type. Fix: change Optional[Dict[str, bool]] to Optional[Dict[str, Any]] (preferred — matches loose-value semantics already used elsewhere in the codebase). No DB migration needed.
